title: 001 checklist never synced with implementation — 80+ items unchecked
evidence: grep [x] returns 0 hits across 80+ checkpoints. Implementation-summary claims Phases A, A.1, B1 partial complete with documented deliverables (6 packet docs, tx-model ADR, memory.md + yaml template) but no checklist items were ever marked.
recommendation: Update checklist to reflect actual delivery state; mark items with evidence references; establish rule that checklist updates accompany implementation-summary writes.

title: 002 checklist 96% unchecked despite claimed 95% completion
evidence: Only 3 of 75+ items checked (CHK-001..003 pre-impl). All scenario, root playbook, sandbox harness, smoke test, and close items are unchecked. But evidence on disk confirms 23 scenarios, 31 sandbox files, root playbook integration, and 5/7 gates pass.
recommendation: Run grep [ ] 002/checklist.md and update every item where evidence exists on disk to [x] with evidence path. Leave incomplete items unchecked with deferred rationale.

title: YAML asset count mismatch: spec says 10, resource-map says 5, disk has 5
evidence: Spec REQ-002 and In Scope table state 10 YAML assets. Resource-map lists 5 (one per command). Disk has 5 YAMLs. Implementation-summary Track B1 hints at planned 4-YAML-per-command structure (auto/confirm/apply/apply-confirm) that was never fully authored.
recommendation: Either update spec.md REQ-002 acceptance criterion from 10 to 5 (if intentional scope reduction) and document in decision-record, or author remaining 5 YAML assets.

title: P0 REQ-003 strict validate exit 0 never passed — known cross-packet blocker
evidence: REQ-003 classified P0 in spec.md but validate.sh --strict never exits 0 for 013 packet. implementation-summary G3 is pending. 002/impl notes phase-parent lean-trio detection works but validation exits non-zero. Cross-packet template-manifest mismatch affects 4+ packets. No explicit deferral ADR in decision-record.
recommendation: Record formal ADR deferring REQ-003 until cross-packet template-manifest alignment fix lands, with user approval; or address 4 error categories at packet level.

title: No POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW evidence for either 001 or 002 packet
evidence: CHK-803 (001) and CHK-503 (002) require POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW after /memory:save. Neither implementation-summary contains POST-SAVE string or documents the review output. description.json and graph-metadata.json exist but the mandatory post-save review step is undocumented.
recommendation: Run /memory:save with generate-context.js for both packets, capture POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW output, address HIGH issues, document in implementation-summary.

title: update.md has zero REQ-* identifier references — no bidirectional spec→code traceability
evidence: grep -c REQ-00[1-8]|REQ-01[0-7] update.md returns 0. All 256 lines of the orchestrator command lack inline spec requirement references. Implementation exists but mapping is implicit.
recommendation: Optionally add inline REQ-ID annotations in future doc polish passes. Not blocking.

title: implementation-summary G1 verification claim (1/5) understates actual file delivery
evidence: G1 status says 1 of 5 but all 5 command .md files exist on disk (memory.md 272 loc, causal-graph.md 271 loc, deep-loop.md 262 loc, cocoindex.md 256 loc, update.md 256 loc). Verification tool was simply not run on 4 files.
recommendation: Update G1 status to 5 files authored, validation run on 1/5 rather than 1 of 5 which implies only 1 file was created.

evidence: 8/8 P0 reqs in 001 have impl files; 12/12 P0 reqs in 002 have impl evidence. Gaps: REQ-002 YAML count mismatch (10→5), REQ-003 validate never passed, no REQ-ID back-refs in commands. P1 reqs all have evidence on disk.
001: 0/80+ checked. 002: 3/75+ checked. Evidence exists on disk for most items but never linked.
002 resource-map entries match disk reality. 001 resource-map has YAML count discrepancy (R6-P1-003) and stale PLANNED annotations for JSON metadata.
23 scenarios exist, root playbook has 23 Section 12 entries, sandbox scripts pass bash -n, docker-compose is valid YAML.

rationale: Implementation delivery substantially complete on disk but paper trail (checklist, spec_code bidirectional refs, POST-SAVE review) has never been maintained.
